Subsea Practice Test Day 3 Questions: What type of drilling conditions make kick detection most difficult for drillers?
(A): Low permeability formations with oil-based muds showing delayed indicators
(B): High permeability sands with water-based muds and fast flow
(C): Deep drilling operations with high mud weights and solid control
(D): Shallow formations with low pressures and minimal underbalance

Subsea Practice Test Day 3 Questions: What information does slow circulating rate pressure provide for well control planning?
(D): System friction at various rates enabling accurate initial circulating pressure calculations

Subsea Practice Test Day 3 Questions: What is the relationship between bottom hole pressure and wellbore stability?
(A): BHP has no effect on wellbore stability
(D): BHP must be maintained between pore pressure and fracture pressure for stability

Subsea Practice Test Day 3 Questions: What is the most critical concern when gas accumulates in the riser after well shut-in?
(A): Gas expansion can create unstable fluid columns and potential gas locks in deepwater
(B): Gas increases mud weight requiring lower pump pressure
(C): Gas improves circulation efficiency through the riser system
(D): Gas prevents hydrate formation in choke and kill lines

Subsea Practice Test Day 3 Questions: What is the goal of the first circulation in Driller's Method?
(C): To circulate influx out with original mud while maintaining constant bottom hole pressure
(D): To reduce all surface pressures to zero
